text.title: Cost-Quality Pareto Output Contract
text.summary: A Pareto eval produces three artifacts: the fixture archive, the results CSV, and the decision memo. All three must be archived together for the decision to be replayable.

list.item: fixtures-vN.json — frozen fixture set with version number.

results-yyyymmdd.csv — per-call rows: fixture_id, candidate, input_tokens, output_tokens, cost_usd, latency_ms, cache_hit, score_correctness, score_faithfulness, score_helpfulness, score_format, raw_output.
pareto-yyyymmdd.png — chart with cost (x), quality (y), each candidate's point, frontier marked.
decision-memo.md — pinned decision + reason + replay metadata.
trade_reason: why this frontier point and not another.
replay_metadata: fixture-set version, eval date, prompt SHA, raters.
review_trigger: when to re-run (new model release / 6 months / cost target shift).

antipattern.reason: Violates r1. Comparison is invalid; results CSV must show all candidates ran on the same fixture set version.

antipattern.description: Decision picks a dominated candidate without explicit override reason.
antipattern.reason: Violates r5. Either the frontier was misread, or there's a hidden constraint that should be documented (e.g., "opus required for safety review" → make that a quality dimension instead).

antipattern.description: Single-rater LLM-as-judge scoring with no human spot-check.
antipattern.reason: LLM-as-judge has known biases (verbosity, self-preference). At minimum, a 10% human spot-check is required.

antipattern.description: Cost reported using published per-1k-token rate without accounting for caching used in production.
antipattern.reason: Violates r3. Production cost ≠ list rate when caching is enabled.

list.item: Fixture set frozen and versioned before run.
All candidates ran on the same fixture-set version.
Cost includes actual production-mode (cached vs uncached) usage.
Decision memo cites a frontier candidate (or has an explicit override reason).
Replay metadata complete; review trigger set.
